JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Specializes in one or more of the following areas: Hematology Testing cell-related components of blood samples to enumerate and identify their cellular components, including the microscopic evaluation of blood smears and the use of automated and manual counting techniques. Cytology Processing blood and body fluids for microscopic examination by pathologists.  Includes slide preparation and staining, utilizing manual and automated counting techniques. Urinalysis Analysis and evaluation of chemical and microscopic components of urine samples, using automated instrumentation and manual techniques. Chemistry/Endocrinology Testing blood and body fluids for various analytes, utilizing automated instrumentation. Parasitology Examining samples to identify parasites, utilizing fecal flotation and various microscopic techniques. Immunology/Serology Testing blood and other samples for the presence of viral and bacterial disease by detecting antibodies (AB) or antigens (AG). Microbiology Processing clinical samples for the identification of bacteria and fungi using automated and manual methods. PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) Examining samples for the presence of specific substances (often bacteria) using molecular diagnostic methods. Histology Processing tissue samples for Pathologists using grossing, embedding, microtomy, staining and special staining techniques.  Assures accurate and timely delivery of casework (and collateral materials) to on-site & off-site pathologists.  May be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting virtual microscopy (VM) systems which provide remote viewing & digital slide analysis capability to pathologists; organizes and archives pending and completed VM cases; creates and maintains VM system standard operating procedures; tracks VM system errors; troubleshoots VM system problems with pathologists, labs, vendors, IT and others; runs regular meetings and meets with vendors; sets up and trains users; and other related activities. Diagnostic Evaluation Performing postmortem examinations, including sample collection and preparation for serology, microbiology, parasitology, histology, molecular biology (PCR) and other tests.  Preparing samples for analysis and performs parasitological and other exams Performing variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers, providing information, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction. Responding to customer phone and other communications, addresses questions/issues/problems/concerns, etc. Reliable and dependable attendance is an essential function of the position. Adherence to attendance-related policies and expectations is critical to this position. EDUCATION: High School diploma or equivalent combination of education and experience required. Bachelors degree or equivalent preferred. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Laboratory skills, with experience setting up, running and reading lab tests and operating lab instruments. General science background, including hematology/chemistry helpful. Attention to detail. Organized with ability to multi-task in a fast paced environment. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Communication skills, both verbal and written. Positive, can-do attitude. Knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) as appropriate. Supervisory skills, if a lead position. Personal computer skills, including strong typing ability and proficient use of Microsoft Office. CERTIFICATIONS: Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), Certified Veterinary Technician (CVT), Licensed Veterinary Technician (LVT), or equivalent preferred. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, phone and computer use. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. Extended hours may be required. Some travel required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: Laboratory environment with potential biohazards present. Potential for exposure to agents known to cause zoonotic disease in humans and use of potentially hazardous chemicals as defined by the National Hazard Communication Standards. A complete list of such chemicals is available from department supervision. LEVELING GUIDE: Sets up and runs lab tests.  Reads and releases test results.  Tests performed may be more routine and less complex.  Exercises judgment in evaluating and reporting results in consultation with veterinarians/pathologists and others.  Performs equipment maintenance activities.  Knowledge of lab equipment and procedures.  Awareness of quality control and calibration.  Developing knowledge of multiple specialties.  Works under close to moderate supervision with limited latitude for independent judgment.  Consult with senior peers on non-complex tasks to learn through experience.  Normally receives general instructions on routine work, detailed instructions on new assignments.  Basic skills and proficiency (may sacrifice speed for accuracy until proficiency grows).  Applies job skills and lab policies and procedures to complete a variety of tasks.  Works on tasks that are semi-routine in nature where ability to recognize deviation from accepted practice is required.  May be cross trained and perform specimen processing responsibilities.  Certification as a Veterinary Technician (RVT, LVT, CVT – Registered, Licensed or Certified through American Veterinary Medical Association accredited program-AVMA) or Medical Laboratory Technician (MLT) (American Society of Clinical Pathology-ASCP), Histotechnician for histology-specific (HT) (ASCP) or military equivalent a plus. For customer service-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned), familiarity with customer service, technically-related fields, veterinary practices or lab experience preferred. For virtual microscopy (VM)-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned),   familiarity with VM equipment, answers basic and escalates more complex questions and troubleshoots basic equipment repairs.  Developing ability at diagnosing, trouble-shooting and resolving technical issues. Typically 0-1+ Year Exp.
JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Performs laboratory analyses and tests. Prepares samples, sets up instruments, runs tests and reads, interprets and releases results. May perform variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Increase the diagnostic revenue year over year in a specific territory Translate customer needs into diagnostic solutions. Sell diagnostic equipment, tests and services to customers. Increase utilization of in-house diagnostic tests & assays, instrument consumables and reference laboratory profiles & advanced tests for territory customer base. Achieve quarterly and annual revenue and growth goals through new account acquisition and existing account development. Utilize IDEXX specialists to assist with acquiring new business as appropriate Educate and train veterinary clinic staff on products, services & marketing initiatives, including elevating issues to customer service when appropriate. Present IDEXX values to customers in a professional manner; including understanding the industry, veterinary practice, customers, technology, operations, and processes. Complete all sales administration, including completing all Beacon requirements & territory management activities. Attend trade shows and other educational events. Achieve and maintain medical & technical knowledge and verbal fluency of industry and IDEXX products and services. Manage territory for optimal account coverage, including quality account planning. Define and verify customer needs and expectations. Communicate customer needs/issues to marketing organization and business unit manager, including recommendations of modifying service levels to current accounts. Communicate competitive issues to marketing organization and business unit manager. Partner, coordinate and assist laboratory operations to facilitate/handle customer questions and/or service issues. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Demonstrated strong sales and closing skills. Documented track record of success in achieving sales goals. Strong communication skills with the ability to influence and negotiate with customers, co-workers and peers. Demonstrated ability to develop, grow and manage customer relationships. Customer Relationship Management skills a plus Ability to understand customer needs and develop customer specific solutions. Utilizes creativity in problem solution, works easily with team partners to solve customer needs. Must be professionally mature, and able to adapt to independent and team-oriented environment. Must possess a passion for business with the drive to achieve results. Integrity, keeping commitments to colleagues, customers and the company. Able to demonstrate modeling and authentic support for IDEXX mission, values and principles. Solid computer skills and ability to utilize sales information management databases. Strong interpersonal skills – ability to build strong customer relationships, and build outstanding relationships with customers & colleagues. Strong collaboration & teamwork skills. Detailed and organized with the ability to multi-task. Solid analytical skills with the ability to analyze and understand customer sales data. Knowledge of veterinary industry and/or veterinary diagnostics preferred. Must possess a valid driver’s license. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Overnight travel Some weekend work Extensive car travel Ability to lift up to 60 pounds WORK ENVIRONMENT: Exposure to veterinary clinics LEVELING GUIDE: Requires basic and developing sales and customer relationship skills. Requires introductory knowledge of IDEXX products and services. Builds and develops strong customer relationships. Critical thinking, evaluation and judgement skills are evolving. Can bring additional perspective and depth to thought process. Building analytical skills and ability to think more broadly. Developing ability to incorporate multiple viewpoints. Complexity in low to moderate range. Able to think of and consider somewhat wider range of solutions and options to address problems. Typically involved in assignments of shorter duration involving fewer people and resources. Works independently on problems of limited to moderate scope. Scientific medical and/or technical background, training, and/or experience a plus. Experience in a Veterinary Clinic a plus.

Acquire new business in existing customer base and acquire new customers leading to an overall increase in the territory revenue. Help build customer loyalty through the effective use of IDEXX products & services including in-house and reference laboratory diagnostic solutions in the customer’s practice. The selling focus will be on a customer centric approach to help practices grow their diagnostic revenue with IDEXX as a high value partner.

Knowledge of security administration and processes across multiple systems and applications including but not limited to SAP, systems, applications, information, infrastructure, middleware, etc. Thorough knowledge in security infrastructure with multiple project implementations. Able to effectively learn and work in a virtual environment, with minimal supervision . Demonstrates flexibility to ensure seamless service operations are maintained . A high level of importance is placed on quality and service.  As the veterinary specialist develops in this role, global and strategic responsibilities increase.

Experience with Advanced Business Applications Programming (ABAP), Java, Visual Basic and/or other object oriented and procedural programming languages as appropriate to specialty. Familiarity with applications development process. Familiarity with software development methodologies as required by associated development team. General business knowledge required. Excellent problem solving skills required. Excellent customer service and business relationship-building skills required. Project management skills required. Microsoft Desktop tools Ability to work independently and in teams. Fluency in the English language. Strong communication skills, both verbal and written, including ability to translate technical subject matter to non-technical audiences (both as a speaker and listener). PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, phone and computer use. Some travel may be required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: General office environment. Normal office noise level, with occasional moderate noise. JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Builds computer applications to meet business informational needs and requirements. Designs, codes, tests, debugs and documents programs based on technical configurations developed by analysts and project teams. Supports and troubleshoots existing applications. Evaluates and analyzes business requirements for new systems and system changes.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Provide interpretation of radiographs, ultrasound, MRI and CT cases and preparation of imaging reports in a timely manner Meet caseload and turnaround expectations. Provide telephone consultations to clients regarding case follow-up, special procedures, positioning and recommended diagnostic imaging studies. Consult with and assist other pathologists as needed to promote collegiality. Promote the growth of IDEXX by actively participating in specialist meetings and developing and maintaining professional relationships/contact with clients Participate in the quality control programs. Maintain proficiency and develop diagnostic skills through regular continuing education Participate in the development of new procedures to continually improve client service and diagnostic yield. Other duties as assigned. Adheres to and models the IDEXX Purpose & Guiding Principles. For some individuals assigned to specific roles through an application process, duties may be assigned in addition to the duties outlined above and may account for a proportion of the total work performed.  These duties may fall within the following areas: Mentorship, Quality, and Training Lead - Provide ongoing support for the medical manager to improve employee engagement, productivity, and performance whilst providing the most accurate diagnoses for our clients. Assist with on boarding of new hires, including but not limited to providing support on work flow optimization, report style, and difficult cases. Act as a mentor for the team, providing support and coaching for employee radiologists Other duties as assigned Special Projects – Directly assist the Telemedicine Medical leadership in the following functions: Work within Telemedicine or across IDEXX in support of software development, including testing new technologies and participating in implementation/training. Work with the CMO, Commercial Learning or Digital Imaging to provide support for customer facing activities to strengthen the business, including: presentations, publications, client education materials University/Specialty Practice Liaison – Provide research, educational or clinical support of academic programs One-year fellowship with an educational or research focus to advance personal or professional knowledge, aligning with IDEXX interests. Shared position to support educational (veterinary students or residents) or clinical needs University or Specialty practice Locums to support clinical needs and build/strengthen relationships EDUCATION: DVM or equivalent veterinary degree required REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Familiarity with current topics and issues in clinical veterinary medicine. Attends and participates in pathology service meetings. Participates in laboratory quality control program. Develops proficiency and diagnostic skills. Ability to type and use dictation equipment. Excellent verbal and communication skills. A service oriented attitude. Ability to organize and prioritize work. Computer skills are highly desired. Occasionally required to climb, balance, bend, stoop, kneel or crouch. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. Extended hours may be required. Some travel required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: Laboratory environment with potential biohazards present. Excellent verbal and written communications skills; ability to present and explain information in a way that establishes rapport, persuades others and gains understanding. JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Prepares materials, performs experiments, gathers data, carries out analysis and reaches conclusions. May also design experiments. Documents experiments and other lab or production activities. Prepares reports and summaries. Presents results and conclusions. Maintains appropriate records. Participates on and may manage teams, planning and organizing team activities. Orders and maintains equipment and work area. Collaborates and communicates with colleagues and others in related areas to provide work support, exchange information and ensure efficient processes. May produce and/or test reagents and final products and perform other production-related activities. Maintains knowledge of products and procedures, developments in the field and industry and regulatory environment. Adheres to appropriate procedures and regulatory requirements. May participate in conferences, webinars and seminars to continue to develop knowledge base. If working in an ISO 17025 accredited laboratory will follow the Water Laboratory Quality Manual and all associated documents. Adheres to and models the IDEXX Purpose & Guiding Principles. EDUCATION: Bachelors degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Specific technical skills related to the applicable scientific specialty. Laboratory bench and instrumentation skills. Analytical and problem solving skills. Strong communication skills, both verbal and written. Ability to work independently and cooperatively as part of a team. Organizational skills and ability to plan work. Takes initiative and is accountable (shows ownership); focuses on task at hand; detail oriented. Adaptable. Ability to work well under pressure and handle the stress of deadlines. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, standing and computer use. Extend and reach with hands and arms and use hands and fingers. Occasionally required to climb, balance, bend, stoop, kneel or crouch. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Some travel may be required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: Laboratory setting with varying levels of noise and exposure to various potential chemical and biological hazards. Compliance with approved laboratory practices is required. LEVELING GUIDE: Requires knowledge and experience in own discipline; acquiring higher level knowledge and skills. Builds knowledge of the organization, processes and customers. Works on problems of more varied scope where analysis of situations or data requires a review of a variety of factors. Exercises judgment within defined procedures and practices to determine appropriate action. Works on a weekly to monthly timeframe with limited supervision. Multi experiment approach. Works autonomously within established procedures and practices and may help determine appropriate approach for new assignments. Shares information with others so there are no surprises. Involves others as appropriate to accomplish individual and group goals. Facilitates an open dialog with a wide variety of contributors and stakeholders. Clear, concise, and professional in communication. Actively listens with interest to what others have to say and checks for understanding. Presents to larger audiences and data reviews. Suggests creative ideas and innovative solutions; introduces new ways of looking at problems. Explores multiple alternatives and approaches to overcome obstacles and find solutions. Actively seeks input from pertinent sources to make timely and well-informed decisions. Demonstrates wisdom and good judgment in routine, day-to-day decision making. Makes sound independent decisions in urgent and non-routine situations. Skillfully separates opinions from facts. Sets aggressive goals and has high standards. Demonstrates a strong drive to achieve meaningful results. Drives tasks to successful completion and closure. Is honest and straightforward when working with others. Honors agreements and commitments, even when working through competing priorities. Expresses self in credible and transparent manner. Addresses process breakdowns with speed and thoroughness. Uses process analysis methods like metrics and benchmarks. Thinks about the whole system. Aligns own work plans and priorities with those of other units. Balances the need for adequate planning with the need for action. Stages activities with relevant milestones and schedules. Anticipates and adjusts effective contingency plans. Demonstrates flexibility of responses to different situations. Recognizes cues that suggest a change in approach or behavior is needed. Cultivates adaptive behavior in others. Demonstrates strong technical knowledge of the IDEXX processes and protocols for their area of responsibility. Can explain things and impart confidence in others. Becomes a valuable asset to others’ projects. Can independently present to a technical board. Demonstrates strong knowledge and responsibility – has ability to perform under pressure. May be mentoring others or have a direct report. Demonstrates advanced lab techniques, instrument maintenance and troubleshooting. Able to teach others. Recognizes areas for improvement in current/proposed processes, and makes suggestions for changes. More knowledge about products and procedures. Works within time constraints. Creates new protocols. Involved in decision making as part of discussion, making recommendations and providing data. Works on design and execution of multifactorial experiments or lead an 8D or work on multiple manufacturing projects simultaneously with the guidance of a mentor. Complexity of experiments or reagent manufacturing protocols increasing. Multi-tasking assignments are expected. Comfortable with moderately complex data sets. Root cause could be vague – knows what data means and what to do with it. Ability to understand, interpret and discuss data. Provides data and makes recommendations. Performs statistical analysis/predictive modeling, reagent analytics/ specifications. Ability to analyze (split complex problems into set of simple problems). Strong documentation skills. Contributing to Design History Record or New Product Transfer documentation.
JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Performs a variety of scientific activities to support the development, maintenance, improvement and understanding of our products and reagent production processes.  Participates on a technically-focused team as an approach to define technical solutions contributing work requiring the consistent exercise of discretion and judgment. Executes experiments and/or follows standard protocols at the laboratory bench to inform decisions or recommendations. Uses advanced knowledge to analyze, interpret or make deductions from varying facts or circumstances. Prepares solutions and reagents. Communicates and presents to colleagues. May specialize in New Product Development, Research, Reagent Manufacture, Process Development, Product Support or Systems. Degree of complexity and autonomy is dependent on level of experience.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Specializes in one or more of the following areas: Hematology Testing cell-related components of blood samples to enumerate and identify their cellular components, including the microscopic evaluation of blood smears and the use of automated and manual counting techniques. Cytology Processing blood and body fluids for microscopic examination by pathologists.  Includes slide preparation and staining, utilizing manual and automated counting techniques. Urinalysis Analysis and evaluation of chemical and microscopic components of urine samples, using automated instrumentation and manual techniques. Chemistry/Endocrinology Testing blood and body fluids for various analytes, utilizing automated instrumentation. Parasitology Examining samples to identify parasites, utilizing fecal flotation and various microscopic techniques. Immunology/Serology Testing blood and other samples for the presence of viral and bacterial disease by detecting antibodies (AB) or antigens (AG). Microbiology Processing clinical samples for the identification of bacteria and fungi using automated and manual methods. PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) Examining samples for the presence of specific substances (often bacteria) using molecular diagnostic methods. Histology Processing tissue samples for Pathologists using grossing, embedding, microtomy, staining and special staining techniques.  Assures accurate and timely delivery of casework (and collateral materials) to on-site & off-site pathologists.  May be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting virtual microscopy (VM) systems which provide remote viewing & digital slide analysis capability to pathologists; organizes and archives pending and completed VM cases; creates and maintains VM system standard operating procedures; tracks VM system errors; troubleshoots VM system problems with pathologists, labs, vendors, IT and others; runs regular meetings and meets with vendors; sets up and trains users; and other related activities. Diagnostic Evaluation Performing postmortem examinations, including sample collection and preparation for serology, microbiology, parasitology, histology, molecular biology (PCR) and other tests.  Preparing samples for analysis and performs parasitological and other exams Performing variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers, providing information, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction. Responding to customer phone and other communications, addresses questions/issues/problems/concerns, etc. Reliable and dependable attendance is an essential function of the position. Adherence to attendance-related policies and expectations is critical to this position. EDUCATION: High School diploma or equivalent combination of education and experience required. Bachelors degree or equivalent preferred. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Laboratory skills, with experience setting up, running and reading lab tests and operating lab instruments. General science background, including hematology/chemistry helpful. Attention to detail. Organized with ability to multi-task in a fast paced environment. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Communication skills, both verbal and written. Positive, can-do attitude. Knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) as appropriate. Supervisory skills, if a lead position. Personal computer skills, including strong typing ability and proficient use of Microsoft Office. CERTIFICATIONS: Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), Certified Veterinary Technician (CVT), Licensed Veterinary Technician (LVT), or equivalent preferred. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, phone and computer use. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Specializes in one or more of the following areas: Hematology Testing cell-related components of blood samples to enumerate and identify their cellular components, including the microscopic evaluation of blood smears and the use of automated and manual counting techniques. Specifics for LPD                                                                                                                                                     

The LPD Professional Services Veterinarian consults with diagnostic livestock laboratories, livestock producers, general livestock veterinary practices, specialty practices, and academic veterinarians on medical protocols to promote growth and utilization of IDEXX products, services, and medical testing; and to promote customer satisfaction and loyalty. The LPD PSV represents IDEXX to the livestock industry, the veterinary community and academia. The LPD PSV partners with the LPD salesforce and collaborates with internal teams providing medical messaging, medical product training to sales and marketing (results interpretation, sampling, etc.) and managing field trials. The LPD PSV produces results and data analysis and interpretation to show the value of the diagnostic tools to producers and veterinarians.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Specializes in one or more of the following areas: Hematology Testing cell-related components of blood samples to enumerate and identify their cellular components, including the microscopic evaluation of blood smears and the use of automated and manual counting techniques. Cytology Processing blood and body fluids for microscopic examination by pathologists.  Includes slide preparation and staining, utilizing manual and automated counting techniques. Urinalysis Analysis and evaluation of chemical and microscopic components of urine samples, using automated instrumentation and manual techniques. Chemistry/Endocrinology Testing blood and body fluids for various analytes, utilizing automated instrumentation. Parasitology Examining samples to identify parasites, utilizing fecal flotation and various microscopic techniques. Immunology/Serology Testing blood and other samples for the presence of viral and bacterial disease by detecting antibodies (AB) or antigens (AG). Microbiology Processing clinical samples for the identification of bacteria and fungi using automated and manual methods. PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) Examining samples for the presence of specific substances (often bacteria) using molecular diagnostic methods. Histology Processing tissue samples for Pathologists using grossing, embedding, microtomy, staining and special staining techniques.  Assures accurate and timely delivery of casework (and collateral materials) to on-site & off-site pathologists.  May be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting virtual microscopy (VM) systems which provide remote viewing & digital slide analysis capability to pathologists; organizes and archives pending and completed VM cases; creates and maintains VM system standard operating procedures; tracks VM system errors; troubleshoots VM system problems with pathologists, labs, vendors, IT and others; runs regular meetings and meets with vendors; sets up and trains users; and other related activities. Diagnostic Evaluation Performing postmortem examinations, including sample collection and preparation for serology, microbiology, parasitology, histology, molecular biology (PCR) and other tests.  Preparing samples for analysis and performs parasitological and other exams Performing variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers, providing information, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction. Responding to customer phone and other communications, addresses questions/issues/problems/concerns, etc. Reliable and dependable attendance is an essential function of the position. Adherence to attendance-related policies and expectations is critical to this position. EDUCATION: High School diploma or equivalent combination of education and experience required. Bachelors degree or equivalent preferred. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Laboratory skills, with experience setting up, running and reading lab tests and operating lab instruments. General science background, including hematology/chemistry helpful. Attention to detail. Organized with ability to multi-task in a fast paced environment. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Communication skills, both verbal and written. Positive, can-do attitude. Knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) as appropriate. Supervisory skills, if a lead position. Personal computer skills, including strong typing ability and proficient use of Microsoft Office. CERTIFICATIONS: Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), Certified Veterinary Technician (CVT), Licensed Veterinary Technician (LVT), or equivalent preferred. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, phone and computer use. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. Extended hours may be required. Some travel required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: Laboratory environment with potential biohazards present. Potential for exposure to agents known to cause zoonotic disease in humans and use of potentially hazardous chemicals as defined by the National Hazard Communication Standards. A complete list of such chemicals is available from department supervision. LEVELING GUIDE: Sets up and runs lab tests.  Reads and releases test results.  Tests performed may be more routine and less complex.  Exercises judgment in evaluating and reporting results in consultation with veterinarians/pathologists and others.  Performs equipment maintenance activities.  Knowledge of lab equipment and procedures.  Awareness of quality control and calibration.  Developing knowledge of multiple specialties.  Works under close to moderate supervision with limited latitude for independent judgment.  Consult with senior peers on non-complex tasks to learn through experience.  Normally receives general instructions on routine work, detailed instructions on new assignments.  Basic skills and proficiency (may sacrifice speed for accuracy until proficiency grows).  Applies job skills and lab policies and procedures to complete a variety of tasks.  Works on tasks that are semi-routine in nature where ability to recognize deviation from accepted practice is required.  May be cross trained and perform specimen processing responsibilities.  Certification as a Veterinary Technician (RVT, LVT, CVT – Registered, Licensed or Certified through American Veterinary Medical Association accredited program-AVMA) or Medical Laboratory Technician (MLT) (American Society of Clinical Pathology-ASCP), Histotechnician for histology-specific (HT) (ASCP) or military equivalent a plus. For customer service-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned), familiarity with customer service, technically-related fields, veterinary practices or lab experience preferred. For virtual microscopy (VM)-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned),   familiarity with VM equipment, answers basic and escalates more complex questions and troubleshoots basic equipment repairs.  Developing ability at diagnosing, trouble-shooting and resolving technical issues. Typically 0-1+ Year Exp.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Provides on-site customer support related to Company product or service installation, training and/or troubleshooting according to established policies and procedures and applicable regulatory guidelines. Installs equipment at customer locations. Assembles components and tests operation. Adjusts equipment as needed, coordinating with in-house resources and others. Trains customers in the use of Company products, equipment and services. Provides training in a variety of formats, as well as documentation and other materials. Ensures customer satisfaction in the use and capability of Company products and services. Troubleshoots customer problems and issues with Company products and services. Diagnoses and fixes problems with assistance of in-house technical resources and others as needed. Plans and implements strategies for addressing more complex issues as needed. Provides support for new customer installations and go-live events, responding to any issues that arise and coordinating resolution to ensure smooth customer experience. Communicates customer needs for additional products and services to sales team, research & development and others. Coordinates other teams and additional resources to address customer issues as needed. Ensures ongoing customer satisfaction. Researches customers prior to site visits to obtain information and insight to anticipate issues and enhance the overall customer experience. Details benefits of additional Company products and services as appropriate. Documents customer visits and interactions. EDUCATION: High School degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required. Associates degree or equivalent preferred. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Technical skills to troubleshoot and resolve problems with Company products and services. Communication skills, both written and verbal, including presentation skills. Coordination and project management ability. Relationship management skills, including ability to resolve difficult customer situations. Ability to train customers on the use of technical equipment and effective process and workflow. Ability to address and diffuse difficult customer situations Ability to adapt to varying customer situations and through the use of effective listening skills, understand and respond to the unique needs of each customer. Ability to work collaboratively with internal departments to solve problems and address customer needs Good problem solving and research skills. Strong organizational, time management and prioritization skills. PC skills. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Regularly required to stand and sit for extended periods; extend and reach with hands and arms; and use hands and fingers to handle, squeeze, twist and manipulate objects, controls and tools. Occasionally required to climb, balance, bend, stoop, kneel or crouch. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 60 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. Weekend hours and weekday overtime may be required. Extensive overnight travel required. Ability to travel and work internationally. Ability to drive car for extended periods. Works independently on problems of limited to moderate scope. Receives supervision as needed as experience builds.

Coordinates with Company technical and other resources to address customer issues. Supports quality management system and other compliance requirements.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Increase the diagnostic revenue year over year in a specific territory Translate customer needs into diagnostic solutions. Sell diagnostic equipment, tests and services to customers. Increase utilization of in-house diagnostic tests & assays, instrument consumables and reference laboratory profiles & advanced tests for territory customer base. Achieve quarterly and annual revenue and growth goals through new account acquisition and existing account development. Utilize IDEXX specialists to assist with acquiring new business as appropriate Educate and train veterinary clinic staff on products, services & marketing initiatives, including elevating issues to customer service when appropriate. Develop and grow partnerships with veterinary practice staff. Represent IDEXX mission, values and principles in all dealings with customers and markets. Achieve and maintain medical & technical knowledge and verbal fluency of industry and IDEXX products and services. Manage territory for optimal account coverage, including quality account planning. Define and verify customer needs and expectations. Communicate customer needs/issues to marketing organization and business unit manager, including recommendations of modifying service levels to current accounts. Communicate competitive issues to marketing organization and business unit manager. Partner, coordinate and assist laboratory operations to facilitate/handle customer questions and/or service issues. EDUCATION: Bachelors degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Demonstrated strong sales and closing skills. Documented track record of success in achieving sales goals. Strong communication skills with the ability to influence and negotiate with customers, co-workers and peers. Demonstrated ability to develop, grow and manage customer relationships. Customer Relationship Management skills a plus Ability to understand customer needs and develop customer specific solutions. Utilizes creativity in problem solution, works easily with team partners to solve customer needs. Must be professionally mature, and able to adapt to independent and team-oriented environment. Must possess a passion for business with the drive to achieve results. Integrity, keeping commitments to colleagues, customers and the company. Able to demonstrate modeling and authentic support for IDEXX mission, values and principles. Solid computer skills and ability to utilize sales information management databases. Strong interpersonal skills – ability to build strong customer relationships, and build outstanding relationships with customers & colleagues. Strong collaboration & teamwork skills. Detailed and organized with the ability to multi-task. Solid analytical skills with the ability to analyze and understand customer sales data. Knowledge of veterinary industry and/or veterinary diagnostics preferred. Must possess a valid driver’s license. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Overnight travel Some weekend work Extensive car travel Ability to lift up to 60 pounds WORK ENVIRONMENT: Exposure to veterinary clinics LEVELING GUIDE: Highly skilled with solid proficiency in sales and customer relationships. Grows, manages and maintains strong customer relationships. Requires more detailed knowledge and experience. Occasionally contributes to process improvements. Solves moderately complex problems; takes a new perspective using existing solutions. Works independently; receives minimal guidance. Typically 5-8 years of relevant field sales experience required. Scientific medical and/or technical background, training, and/or experience required.
JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Create relationships with customers through knowledgeable interactions, business discussions and educational interactions. Acquire new business in existing customer base and acquire new customers leading to an overall increase in the territory revenue. Help build customer loyalty through the effective use of IDEXX products & services including in-house and reference laboratory diagnostic solutions in the customer’s practice. The selling focus will be on a customer centric approach to help practices grow their diagnostic revenue with IDEXX as a high value partner.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Providing high quality phone consultations to IDEXX customers about diagnostic testing , interpretation of results and clinical application of results in individual diseases and/or patients within their specialty. Recording pertinent information about consultations within a Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system. Ensuring ongoing customer satisfaction and fostering strong customer relationships. Supporting quality management system and other compliance requirements. Collaborating consistently and collegially with IDEXX colleagues both inside and outside immediate team. Participating in IDEXX educational and organization meetings as required . Travelling once or twice a year as needed for training or other purposes. Providing support as needed to other CAG support and commercial groups at IDEXX. Maintaining credentials to practice veterinary medicine as a specialist . EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE : Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) or equivalent required. Board certification in relevant specialty required . ( Consideration given to residency trained candidates with extensive clinical experience or candidates with an advanced degree in relevant specialty.) At least 3 years clinical experience in relevant specialty is strongly preferred. Other relevant IDEXX lab/sales/CTS experience will be considered. Other relevant industry experience will be considered. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Excellent verbal and written communication skills Excellent interpersonal and teamwork skills A collaborative, service-oriented attitude. Familiarity with current topics and issues in clinical veterinary medicine , particularly in area of specialty . Familiarity with current IDEXX POC and IRL test offering in specialty . Excellent reasoning skills. Fluency in local language as well as basic English. In some regions fluency in more than 1 language may be required . Able to effectively learn and work in a virtual environment, with minimal supervision . Demonstrates flexibility to ensure seamless service operations are maintained . Technical competence and ability to learn/adapt to new technology platforms Able to organize and prioritize work. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, telephone headset, and computer use. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate via telephone and in person. Weekend hours may be required . Some travel will be required . WORK ENVIRONMENT: Virtual home office with dedicated quiet space and high-speed internet connection. General office environment with normal office noise level, with occasional moderate noise. LEVELING GUIDE: Level 1 - Veterinary Specialist I : Is a skilled and knowledgeable veterinar y specialist with comprehensive clinical experience and acumen in area of specialty . Understands IDEXX's Mission, vision & values . Understands MCS organizational structure. Has a growth mindset and proactively seeks new opportunities. Thorough knowledge of common IRL and POC IDEXX tests, especially IDEXX proprietary diagnostics . Broad awareness of diagnostic s in area of specialty . Thinks logically to make good decisions. Demonstrates emotional intelligence to build rapport with customers and colleagues . Open to receiving feedback and takes action to address gaps. Working towards and meeting phone targets. Is resilient to change. Is able to use the CRM and telephony systems for capturing call drivers. Is enthusiastic about learning and quick to learn new skills. Completes work with supervision initially, working towards independence.
JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: The primary responsibility for the veterinary specialist in this position is to provide phone consultations to customers about IDEXX Reference Laboratory (IRL) and Point of Care (POC) test results, with emphasis placed on deep understanding of IDEXX diagnostics, interpretation of results, and clinical application to specific diseases and/or patients within their specialty. A high level of importance is placed on quality and service.  As the veterinary specialist develops in this role, global and strategic responsibilities increase.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Specializes in one or more of the following areas: Hematology Testing cell-related components of blood samples to enumerate and identify their cellular components, including the microscopic evaluation of blood smears and the use of automated and manual counting techniques. Cytology Processing blood and body fluids for microscopic examination by pathologists.  Includes slide preparation and staining, utilizing manual and automated counting techniques. Urinalysis Analysis and evaluation of chemical and microscopic components of urine samples, using automated instrumentation and manual techniques. Chemistry/Endocrinology Testing blood and body fluids for various analytes, utilizing automated instrumentation. Parasitology Examining samples to identify parasites, utilizing fecal flotation and various microscopic techniques. Immunology/Serology Testing blood and other samples for the presence of viral and bacterial disease by detecting antibodies (AB) or antigens (AG). Microbiology Processing clinical samples for the identification of bacteria and fungi using automated and manual methods. PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) Examining samples for the presence of specific substances (often bacteria) using molecular diagnostic methods. Histology Processing tissue samples for Pathologists using grossing, embedding, microtomy, staining and special staining techniques.  Assures accurate and timely delivery of casework (and collateral materials) to on-site & off-site pathologists.  May be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting virtual microscopy (VM) systems which provide remote viewing & digital slide analysis capability to pathologists; organizes and archives pending and completed VM cases; creates and maintains VM system standard operating procedures; tracks VM system errors; troubleshoots VM system problems with pathologists, labs, vendors, IT and others; runs regular meetings and meets with vendors; sets up and trains users; and other related activities. Diagnostic Evaluation Performing postmortem examinations, including sample collection and preparation for serology, microbiology, parasitology, histology, molecular biology (PCR) and other tests.  Preparing samples for analysis and performs parasitological and other exams Performing variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers, providing information, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction. Responding to customer phone and other communications, addresses questions/issues/problems/concerns, etc. Reliable and dependable attendance is an essential function of the position. Adherence to attendance-related policies and expectations is critical to this position. EDUCATION: High School diploma or equivalent combination of education and experience required. Bachelors degree or equivalent preferred. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Laboratory skills, with experience setting up, running and reading lab tests and operating lab instruments. General science background, including hematology/chemistry helpful. Attention to detail. Organized with ability to multi-task in a fast paced environment. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Communication skills, both verbal and written. Positive, can-do attitude. Knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) as appropriate. Supervisory skills, if a lead position. Personal computer skills, including strong typing ability and proficient use of Microsoft Office. CERTIFICATIONS: Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), Certified Veterinary Technician (CVT), Licensed Veterinary Technician (LVT), or equivalent preferred. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, phone and computer use. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. Extended hours may be required. Some travel required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: Laboratory environment with potential biohazards present. Potential for exposure to agents known to cause zoonotic disease in humans and use of potentially hazardous chemicals as defined by the National Hazard Communication Standards. A complete list of such chemicals is available from department supervision. LEVELING GUIDE: Sets up and runs lab tests.  Reads and releases test results.  Tests performed may be more routine and less complex.  Exercises judgment in evaluating and reporting results in consultation with veterinarians/pathologists and others.  Performs equipment maintenance activities.  Knowledge of lab equipment and procedures.  Awareness of quality control and calibration.  Developing knowledge of multiple specialties.  Works under close to moderate supervision with limited latitude for independent judgment.  Consult with senior peers on non-complex tasks to learn through experience.  Normally receives general instructions on routine work, detailed instructions on new assignments.  Basic skills and proficiency (may sacrifice speed for accuracy until proficiency grows).  Applies job skills and lab policies and procedures to complete a variety of tasks.  Works on tasks that are semi-routine in nature where ability to recognize deviation from accepted practice is required.  May be cross trained and perform specimen processing responsibilities.  Certification as a Veterinary Technician (RVT, LVT, CVT – Registered, Licensed or Certified through American Veterinary Medical Association accredited program-AVMA) or Medical Laboratory Technician (MLT) (American Society of Clinical Pathology-ASCP), Histotechnician for histology-specific (HT) (ASCP) or military equivalent a plus. For customer service-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned), familiarity with customer service, technically-related fields, veterinary practices or lab experience preferred. For virtual microscopy (VM)-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned),   familiarity with VM equipment, answers basic and escalates more complex questions and troubleshoots basic equipment repairs.  Developing ability at diagnosing, trouble-shooting and resolving technical issues. Typically 0-1+ Year Exp.
JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Performs laboratory analyses and tests. Prepares samples, sets up instruments, runs tests and reads, interprets and releases results. May perform variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers.

Ability to train customers on the use of technical equipment and effective process and workflow. Ability to address and diffuse difficult customer situations Ability to adapt to varying customer situations and through the use of effective listening skills, understand and respond to the unique needs of each customer. Ability to work collaboratively with internal departments to solve problems and address customer needs Good problem solving and research skills. Strong organizational, time management and prioritization skills. PC skills. Works autonomously within established procedures and practices.
JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Provides on-site customer and/or technical support related to Company product or service installation, training and/or troubleshooting. Installs products and services at customer locations, trains customers in their use and investigates and resolves any technical or other problems. Coordinates with Company technical and other resources to address customer issues. Supports quality management system and other compliance requirements.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Increase the diagnostic revenue year over year in a specific territory Translate customer needs into diagnostic solutions. Sell diagnostic equipment, tests and services to customers. Increase utilization of in-house diagnostic tests & assays, instrument consumables and reference laboratory profiles & advanced tests for territory customer base. Achieve quarterly and annual revenue and growth goals through new account acquisition and existing account development. Utilize IDEXX specialists to assist with acquiring new business as appropriate Educate and train veterinary clinic staff on products, services & marketing initiatives, including elevating issues to customer service when appropriate. Develop and grow partnerships with veterinary practice staff. Represent IDEXX mission, values and principles in all dealings with customers and markets. Present IDEXX values to customers in a professional manner; including understanding the industry, veterinary practice, customers, technology, operations, and processes. Complete all sales administration, including completing all Beacon requirements & territory management activities. Attend trade shows and other educational events. Achieve and maintain medical & technical knowledge and verbal fluency of industry and IDEXX products and services. Manage territory for optimal account coverage, including quality account planning. Define and verify customer needs and expectations. Communicate customer needs/issues to marketing organization and business unit manager, including recommendations of modifying service levels to current accounts. Communicate competitive issues to marketing organization and business unit manager. Partner, coordinate and assist laboratory operations to facilitate/handle customer questions and/or service issues. Manage IDEXX expenses and resources in line with company guidelines and directives. EDUCATION: Bachelors degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Demonstrated strong sales and closing skills. Documented track record of success in achieving sales goals. Strong communication skills with the ability to influence and negotiate with customers, co-workers and peers. Demonstrated ability to develop, grow and manage customer relationships. Customer Relationship Management skills a plus Ability to understand customer needs and develop customer specific solutions. Utilizes creativity in problem solution, works easily with team partners to solve customer needs. Must be professionally mature, and able to adapt to independent and team-oriented environment. Must possess a passion for business with the drive to achieve results. Integrity, keeping commitments to colleagues, customers and the company. Able to demonstrate modeling and authentic support for IDEXX mission, values and principles. Solid computer skills and ability to utilize sales information management databases. Strong interpersonal skills – ability to build strong customer relationships, and build outstanding relationships with customers & colleagues. Strong collaboration & teamwork skills. Detailed and organized with the ability to multi-task. Solid analytical skills with the ability to analyze and understand customer sales data. Knowledge of veterinary industry and/or veterinary diagnostics preferred. Must possess a valid driver’s license. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Overnight travel Some weekend work Extensive car travel Ability to lift up to 60 pounds WORK ENVIRONMENT: Exposure to veterinary clinics LEVELING GUIDE: Requires basic and developing sales and customer relationship skills. Requires introductory knowledge of IDEXX products and services. Builds and develops strong customer relationships. Critical thinking, evaluation and judgement skills are evolving. Can bring additional perspective and depth to thought process. Building analytical skills and ability to think more broadly. Developing ability to incorporate multiple viewpoints. Complexity in low to moderate range. Able to think of and consider somewhat wider range of solutions and options to address problems. Typically involved in assignments of shorter duration involving fewer people and resources. Works independently on problems of limited to moderate scope. Receives supervision as needed as experience builds. Typically 1 to 3 years of inside sales experience. Relevant sales experience a plus. Scientific medical and/or technical background, training, and/or experience a plus. Experience in a Veterinary Clinic a plus.
JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Create relationships with customers through knowledgeable interactions, business discussions and educational interactions. Acquire new business in existing customer base and acquire new customers leading to an overall increase in the territory revenue. Help build customer loyalty through the effective use of IDEXX products & services including in-house and reference laboratory diagnostic solutions in the customer’s practice. The selling focus will be on a customer centric approach to help practices grow their diagnostic revenue with IDEXX as a high value partner.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Specializes in one or more of the following areas: Hematology Testing cell-related components of blood samples to enumerate and identify their cellular components, including the microscopic evaluation of blood smears and the use of automated and manual counting techniques. Urinalysis Analysis and evaluation of chemical and microscopic components of urine samples, using automated instrumentation and manual techniques. Chemistry/Endocrinology Testing blood and body fluids for various analytes, utilizing automated instrumentation. Parasitology Examining samples to identify parasites, utilizing fecal flotation and various microscopic techniques. Immunology/Serology Testing blood and other samples for the presence of viral and bacterial disease by detecting antibodies (AB) or antigens (AG). Microbiology Processing clinical samples for the identification of bacteria and fungi using automated and manual methods. PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) Examining samples for the presence of specific substances (often bacteria) using molecular diagnostic methods. Histology Processing tissue samples for Pathologists using grossing, embedding, microtomy, staining and special staining techniques.  Assures accurate and timely delivery of casework (and collateral materials) to on-site & off-site pathologists.  May be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting virtual microscopy (VM) systems which provide remote viewing & digital slide analysis capability to pathologists; organizes and archives pending and completed VM cases; creates and maintains VM system standard operating procedures; tracks VM system errors; troubleshoots VM system problems with pathologists, labs, vendors, IT and others; runs regular meetings and meets with vendors; sets up and trains users; and other related activities. Diagnostic Evaluation Performing postmortem examinations, including sample collection and preparation for serology, microbiology, parasitology, histology, molecular biology (PCR) and other tests.  Preparing samples for analysis and performs parasitological and other exams Performing variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers, providing information, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction. General science background, including hematology/chemistry helpful. Attention to detail. Organized with ability to multi-task in a fast paced environment. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Communication skills, both verbal and written. Positive, can-do attitude. Knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) as appropriate. Supervisory skills, if a lead position. Personal computer skills, including strong typing ability and proficient use of Microsoft Office. CERTIFICATIONS: Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), Certified Veterinary Technician (CVT), Licensed Veterinary Technician (LVT), or equivalent preferred. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, phone and computer use. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. Extended hours may be required. Some travel required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: Laboratory environment with potential biohazards present. A complete list of such chemicals is available from department supervision. JOB_PROFILE_SUMMARY: Performs laboratory analyses and tests. Prepares samples, sets up instruments, runs tests and reads, interprets and releases results. May perform variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Specializes in one or more of the following areas: Hematology Testing cell-related components of blood samples to enumerate and identify their cellular components, including the microscopic evaluation of blood smears and the use of automated and manual counting techniques. Cytology Processing blood and body fluids for microscopic examination by pathologists.  Includes slide preparation and staining, utilizing manual and automated counting techniques. Urinalysis Analysis and evaluation of chemical and microscopic components of urine samples, using automated instrumentation and manual techniques. Chemistry/Endocrinology Testing blood and body fluids for various analytes, utilizing automated instrumentation. Parasitology Examining samples to identify parasites, utilizing fecal flotation and various microscopic techniques. Immunology/Serology Testing blood and other samples for the presence of viral and bacterial disease by detecting antibodies (AB) or antigens (AG). Microbiology Processing clinical samples for the identification of bacteria and fungi using automated and manual methods. PCR (Polymerase Chain Reaction) Examining samples for the presence of specific substances (often bacteria) using molecular diagnostic methods. Histology Processing tissue samples for Pathologists using grossing, embedding, microtomy, staining and special staining techniques.  Assures accurate and timely delivery of casework (and collateral materials) to on-site & off-site pathologists.  May be responsible for maintaining and troubleshooting virtual microscopy (VM) systems which provide remote viewing & digital slide analysis capability to pathologists; organizes and archives pending and completed VM cases; creates and maintains VM system standard operating procedures; tracks VM system errors; troubleshoots VM system problems with pathologists, labs, vendors, IT and others; runs regular meetings and meets with vendors; sets up and trains users; and other related activities. Diagnostic Evaluation Performing postmortem examinations, including sample collection and preparation for serology, microbiology, parasitology, histology, molecular biology (PCR) and other tests.  Preparing samples for analysis and performs parasitological and other exams Performing variety of customer service activities for internal and external customers, providing information, resolving issues and ensuring customer satisfaction. Responding to customer phone and other communications, addresses questions/issues/problems/concerns, etc. Reliable and dependable attendance is an essential function of the position. Adherence to attendance-related policies and expectations is critical to this position. EDUCATION: High School diploma or equivalent combination of education and experience required. Bachelors degree or equivalent preferred. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Laboratory skills, with experience setting up, running and reading lab tests and operating lab instruments. General science background, including hematology/chemistry helpful. Attention to detail. Organized with ability to multi-task in a fast paced environment. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Communication skills, both verbal and written. Positive, can-do attitude. Knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP) as appropriate. Supervisory skills, if a lead position. Personal computer skills, including strong typing ability and proficient use of Microsoft Office. CERTIFICATIONS: Registered Veterinary Technician (RVT), Certified Veterinary Technician (CVT), Licensed Veterinary Technician (LVT), or equivalent preferred. PHYSICAL DEMANDS: Extensive sitting, phone and computer use. May be required to lift, move and carry up to 50 pounds. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, color vision, depth perception, and the ability to adjust focus. Hearing acuity sufficient to effectively communicate with customers and Employees via telephone and in person. Ability to communicate verbally on phone and in person. Fluency in the English language. Extended hours may be required. Some travel required. WORK ENVIRONMENT: Laboratory environment with potential biohazards present. Potential for exposure to agents known to cause zoonotic disease in humans and use of potentially hazardous chemicals as defined by the National Hazard Communication Standards. A complete list of such chemicals is available from department supervision. LEVELING GUIDE: Sets up and runs lab tests.  Reads and releases test results.  Tests performed may be more routine and less complex.  Exercises judgment in evaluating and reporting results in consultation with veterinarians/pathologists and others.  Performs equipment maintenance activities.  Knowledge of lab equipment and procedures.  Awareness of quality control and calibration.  Developing knowledge of multiple specialties.  Works under close to moderate supervision with limited latitude for independent judgment.  Consult with senior peers on non-complex tasks to learn through experience.  Normally receives general instructions on routine work, detailed instructions on new assignments.  Basic skills and proficiency (may sacrifice speed for accuracy until proficiency grows).  Applies job skills and lab policies and procedures to complete a variety of tasks.  Works on tasks that are semi-routine in nature where ability to recognize deviation from accepted practice is required.  May be cross trained and perform specimen processing responsibilities.  Certification as a Veterinary Technician (RVT, LVT, CVT – Registered, Licensed or Certified through American Veterinary Medical Association accredited program-AVMA) or Medical Laboratory Technician (MLT) (American Society of Clinical Pathology-ASCP), Histotechnician for histology-specific (HT) (ASCP) or military equivalent a plus. For customer service-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned), familiarity with customer service, technically-related fields, veterinary practices or lab experience preferred. For virtual microscopy (VM)-related responsibilities (only if these are assigned),   familiarity with VM equipment, answers basic and escalates more complex questions and troubleshoots basic equipment repairs.  Developing ability at diagnosing, trouble-shooting and resolving technical issues. Typically 0-1+ Year Exp.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: May be responsible for any or all of the following: Supports one or several department professionals, division heads, officers, or managers Performs routine tasks including data entry, phone coverage, ordering office supplies, processing and distributing mail, filing, photocopying, processing purchase orders, invoices, check requests and expense reports, preparing reports, meeting minutes and correspondence Coordinates meetings and conference calls Creates and edits documents, spreadsheets and presentations Manages schedules, arranges appointments and itineraries, books airline, hotel and car reservations Remains knowledgeable of business unit policies Manages projects of varying scope and complexity EDUCATION: High School diploma or equivalent combination of education and experience required. Associates degree or equivalent preferred. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Effective communication skills Organized and detail-oriented Familiarity with office equipment Strong computer skills, experience with MS Office – Word, Excel, Outlook, PowerPoint, Visio and SAP preferred Able to work across business units, geographies and functional areas Must demonstrate a high level of service and professionalism Ability to use discretion and handle confidential information Customer-service aptitude; solid interpersonal & communication skills. PHYSICAL DEMANDS Extensive sitting and computer use WORK ENVIRONMENT: Normal office environment LEVELING GUIDE: Has developed skills through training or experience. Performs most established procedures and will typically be considered semi-skilled through on-the-job experience. Work typically involves prescribed processes with opportunity for creativity and judgment within established procedures.

JOB_DESCRIPTION: PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: May be responsible for any or all of the following: Accepts orders for supplies and sample pickups, and assigns to the appropriate driving route Picks up samples and delivers supplies to clients Picks and packs inventory requests for internal and external customers; verifies orders to ensure 100% accuracy Assures inventory levels are accurate; performs cycle counts as required Creates/maintains data warehouse for driver contacts, contract courier contacts and routing information Acts as a liaison for internal and external transportation issue escalation and resolution Acts as point of contact for dispatch, customers, and vendors Maintains clean and orderly warehouse and inventory handling area; maintains a safe working environment and        utilizes applicable safety equipment May lead individuals in daily data entry, customer service and shipping duties EDUCATION: High School degree or equivalent combination of education and experience required. REQUIRED SKILLS AND ABILITIES: Ability to communicate in a clear and professional manner Ability to prioritize and multi-task in a fast-paced environment Ability to work well with others Attention to detail For shipping work: Warehouse and inventory experience is required; experience with forklift operation is required For dispatch work: Customer service experience is required; dispatching and transportation experience is required For delivery work: Must be familiar with, and meet        the minimum driving record requirements as outlined in the IDEXX Safe Driver Program; ability to read maps and maintain a route book are required PHYSICAL DEMANDS: For shipping work: Normal warehouse environment; use of protective equipment and/or clothing may be required; ability to lift up to 60 pounds, repeatedly, required For delivery work: Extensive vehicle travel required WORK ENVIRONMENT: This position involves a potential for exposure to agents known to cause zoonotic disease in humans and use of potentially hazardous chemicals as defined by the OSHA Hazard Communication Standard (29 CFR). A complete list of such chemicals is available from the departmental supervisor. LEVELING GUIDE: Has developed skills through formal        training or considerable work experience. Performs full range of established procedures, and will typically be considered skilled through on-the-job experience. Work typically involves technical skills and/or administrative duties within established procedures with a moderate degree of supervision. Resolves routine issues without supervisory approval.
